title: SW-Forge
rulesExpertBannerText: For players who want deep control: numeric thresholds, role formulas, and Gem/Reapp logic. You need to understand how these settings change verdicts. Dashboard and Rune Table work fine with defaults — skip this unless you really want to dig in.
teamsImportReplaceConfirm: OK = replace all teams. Cancel = merge into existing sets.
monstersIndexMissing: Monster names/icons need data/monsters-index.json (run: node tools/fetch-monsters-index.mjs). Until then only rune sets are shown.
monstersStatSwexNote: Totals from SWEX; base and rune split not in export.
monstersStatsClickHint: Click HP–SPD to switch base+rune / total view
tableScoreHeaderTip: Forge Score: SW Forge combat rating (synergies, archetypes, grinds). Not the in-game Rating column and not the Verdict label.
tableVerdictHeaderTip: Keep / Grind / Sell / Reapp / Gem from your rules. Hover a row tag for that rune’s reason and engine detail.
tableRoleHeaderTip: Build role from stats and sets (e.g. God Roll = rescue line). Used with filters and dashboards — not the same as Verdict.
tableGodPotentialTip: Informational: {stat} is {value} vs God line {line} (not a verdict).
monstersGearEmpty: No artifacts or relics on this monster.
monstersSkillDeficitTip: {n} skill level(s) to max (≈ devilmons if using devils)
monstersSkillsIndexMissing: Skill max levels need data/skills-index.json (run: node tools/fetch-skills-index.mjs). Until then only current levels are shown.
shareViewLoadFailed: Could not load this shared profile. The link may be expired or invalid.
skillPlannerSecondAwakenedOnlyTip: Only monsters whose species can Second Awaken (from monster database). Does not check whether your copy is already 2A.
skillPlannerCdEmpty: No monsters need skill-ups for a Cooltime Turn −1 upgrade.
skillPlannerUpsTip: Skill levels still needed (≈ devilmon uses)
skillPlannerEmptyMaxed: All tracked monsters have maxed skills.
teamsSlotSpd: Combat SPD: base + runes + totem/leader (% of base)
shareLinkCopiedLong: Copied! Send this link to compare builds.
shareNoContent: Nothing to share for this export mode
sharePayloadTooLarge: Export is too large to share ({size} MB). Try equipped-only or a smaller export.
tableShownDetailEmpty: No runes match the current filters.
tableShownDetailCapped: Showing {shown} of {total} matching runes — use Load all above for the full list.
runeTableVerdictHint: Hover a Verdict tag for details.
sellReasonSlowDps: Core DPS stats too low for {role}
sellReasonNearMiss: Close to {role}, preset rules not met
runeTableMoreHintInline: {shown} / {total} in table · load all for full list (large exports may lag)
runeTableMoreHint: For speed, only the first {shown} rows are in the grid. Load all {total} matching runes when you need the full list (very large tables may feel slower).
highRollGridDesc: Stage and grade affect grind targets, rune power (0–3), and formula anchors «High Roll for Hero/Legend». They do not set the God Roll line.
godRollConstantsDesc: Per stat: God line = Base × (1 + God%). The rescue signal (role filter «God Roll») fires when any sub line (roll + grind) reaches that God line — not the HR grid below.
rulesPolicySimpleDashboardNote: Set game stage and Strictness on the Dashboard (progression bar). This tab is for advanced tuning.
rulesPolicySimpleLead: Simple mode: use the Dashboard for stage and Strictness. Open Expert here for Constants, role formulas, and verdict rules.
rulesPolicyExpertLead: Controls how strictly a rune must fit a role. The Sell / Keep / Gem / Grind / Reapp decision tree stays the same.
rulesPageLead: Optional expert area. Constants define numeric thresholds; roles map runes to archetypes. Only change these if you understand the impact — then Save at the bottom to refresh verdicts on the Dashboard and Rune Table.
rulesSectionPreviewsDesc: Read-only previews for the 8 stats above. God Roll: Hero vs Legend. High Roll and Duo Roll: stage × grade (Duo = High Roll × (1 − Duo%)).
constantsSheetDesc: Base is the Mid / Hero anchor for that substat (roll + grind). Percent columns use whole numbers (30 = 30%): God (+%) builds the God Roll line; Duo (−%) scales Duo below High Roll; Early (−%) and Late (+%) scale HR by stage; Legend (−%) lowers Legend vs Hero at Mid. See the three preview blocks below for computed lines. Previews update as you type; Save recalculates verdicts.
enginePreviewDuoHint: Per-stat Duo line = High Roll × (1 − Duo%). Match when any allowed stat pair both meet their Duo lines (spreadsheet pair list).
constantsColHintBase: Starting point for this stat (Mid, Hero). All thresholds scale from here. Uses roll + grind totals. Example: SPD Base 16 means “Mid Hero line starts at 16”.
constantsColHintGod: God threshold (High Value / God). Formula: God = Base × (1 + God%). Example: Base 16 with +25% → 20.
constantsColHintDuo: Duo threshold (High Value / Duo). Formula: Duo = High Roll × (1 − Duo%). Example: HR 16 with −15% → 14.
constantsColHintEarly: Early-game scaling for High Roll. Formula: Early HR = Base × (1 − Early%). Example: Base 16 with 20% → 13.
constantsColHintLate: Late-game scaling for High Roll. Formula: Late HR = Base × (1 + Late%). Example: Base 16 with 30% → 21.
constantsColHintGrade: Legend vs Hero scaling (grade penalty) for High Roll. Formula: Legend Mid HR = Base × (1 − Legend%). Example: Base 16 with 5% → 15.
resetConstantsHint: Reloads the built-in Constants table only. Roles, formulas, and other settings stay unchanged.
resetConstantsConfirm: Replace the Constants (8 stats) grid with built-in defaults? Roles and formulas will not be changed.
configureRoleRules: Configure role rules. You can add a custom role or remove existing roles (minimum one role must remain).
gemMetaRulesDesc: Enchant Gem rerolls substats only (prefix / innate cannot be gemmed). Verdict Gem uses the spreadsheet-style bad-flat sub pattern (clean low flats, no enchantment on subs) plus a plausible grindable % target. Turning this off disables Gem hints.
gemBadFlatHint: Two or more low flat subs early / one in late stage; enchanted subs make the rune “clean”; see engine bad-flat thresholds.
reappDescription: If a rune has low efficiency and matches these filters, verdict can be Reapp.
slotDeleteSwitchedTo: The current database was removed. Loaded Data {n} — {name}.
slotDeleteAllCleared: All saved databases were removed. Please load a JSON export.
slotDeleteNextLoadFailed: Could not load data from the next slot. Please load a JSON file.
stageAdvisorLead: Early, Mid, and Late change how strict the rules are. The combined score estimates account depth from your full rune export (Rare and above): fast runes, upgraded runes, and quality on your best pieces. It ignores the preset below and the dashboard minimum level filter.
stageApplySuggestionAria: Set your preset stage to the suggestion (Early, Mid, or Late) using the dropdown next to Apply.
stageScoreBandsTpl: Recommendation on this score: Mid from {mid} pts, Late from {late}.
stageMetricsExplainer: These three numbers always use your full export — not the preset or Min level below. Card weights:
stageCardKeepDesc: How many 6★ runes are at exactly +15. This term contributes up to 35 points once you reach 600.
stageCardMetaDesc: Average Ingame Score on your best runes (up to 50). Baseline 75, span 10 points for the full 30-point term.
dashboardVerdictStackHint: Click a chart row (Verdict, Roles, Sets, or Slot) to open the Rune Table with that filter.
dashboardTopSpdSlot2Skip: Slot 2 — % main only (SPD subs not ranked)
dashboardTopSpdPotentialHint: →+{n} = SPD if grind reaches +5 on SPD sub lines
dashboardTopSpdSortAria: Sort top runes in each slot column
dashboardUnifiedDistAria: Charts: verdicts, roles, sets, slot mains, or efficiency buckets
